# Research Task 3 — The scripted Hot Ones intro

**Phase:** 1 (run in parallel). **Read-only. Do NOT edit the article.**
**Recommended agent:** research-analyst.

## Why this matters

Evans opens nearly every episode with the same scripted line. The article gestures at "the
voice is the voice, the posture is the posture" but never quotes the actual catchphrase —
which is the _proof_ of the Type 6 point that he narrows to a known, identical-every-time
quantity. The writer needs the verbatim wording.

## What to find (sourced)

1. The exact, current standard opening line (the "Hey, what's going on everybody... for First
   We Feast, I'm Sean Evans and you're watching Hot Ones, the show with hot questions and even
   hotter wings" formula). Confirm the precise current wording.
2. Note if the wording has a known variant or has changed over the run (so the writer doesn't
   quote a stale version).
3. The standard closing/sign-off framing if there's an equally fixed one.

## Deliverable

A short brief (under ~150 words):

- The verbatim intro line in quotes.
- One citation URL (a recent episode transcript or reputable quote source).
- A note on any variant/era differences.

## Guardrails

- Verbatim accuracy is the whole point. If sources disagree, give the most common current form
  and flag the discrepancy.
- Don't touch the article file.
